title: vigna angivensis
intro: Vigna angivensis, also known as the dwarf catechu or climbing wattle, is a perennial climber plant in the legume family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vigna angivensis include lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Vigna angivensis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
